title: Aureline provider sync-health view, replay-blast-radius summary, and degraded-import vocabulary
description: Boundary schema for the provider_sync_health_view_record frozen in docs/providers/provider_sync_health_contract.md. The sync-health view record is the typed snapshot of one (connected provider, object scope) pair's current sync mode, last-success and last-failure provider-event references, cursor / reset state, blast-radius counts by object class, degraded-import entries, replay-blast-radius correlation refs, escalation path, and support-export channels so Aureline never falls back to a generic 'sync failed' label and so imported provider state never masquerades as live truth. The current-mode vocabulary covers live, delayed, partial, replayed, denied, offline, and mirror_derived. The degraded-import vocabulary covers partial, backfilled, mirror_derived, stale_permission, and host_mismatch. The cursor-state vocabulary covers cursor_current, cursor_lagging, cursor_paused, cursor_missing_page_detected, cursor_page_gap_detected, cursor_reset_pending, cursor_reset_in_progress, cursor_reset_completed, and cursor_unknown_repair_required. The failure-class vocabulary covers network_unreachable, credential_expired_or_revoked, host_mismatch, tenant_switch, signature_denied, policy_blocked, rate_limited, cursor_reset_required, missing_page_detected, stale_permission, replay_held, partial_failure, provider_unreachable, and unknown_failure_repair_required. The escalation path enumerates toast_or_banner, detail_view, cli_inspection, support_export, and audit_packet so language travels every consumer. Export channels cover support_bundle, audit_packet, migration_note, admin_assisted_handoff_packet, and object_handoff_packet without revealing raw credentials or tenant-private payloads. Raw URLs, raw tokens, raw callback bodies, raw delegated-token bodies, raw provider payloads, and raw imported-snapshot bodies never cross this boundary; the record carries opaque refs and structured fields only. The eventual provider-mode crate's Rust types are the schema of record; this file is the cross-tool boundary every non-owning surface reads. Adding a new current_mode_class, failure_class, cursor_state_class, degraded_import_class, escalation_surface_class, support_export_channel_class, or object_class is additive-minor and requires a provider_sync_health_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

provider_sync_health_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this provider sync-health vocabulary export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles / activity-center rollups / admin-reconciliation consoles. Raw URLs, raw tokens, raw callback bodies, and raw provider payloads never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short human-readable sentence safe to show in toast / banner copy, detail-view rows, CLI inspection output, support-export packet copy, audit-packet copy, and provider-linked notifications. Raw URLs, raw tokens, raw callback bodies, raw delegated-token bodies, raw provider payloads, and raw imported-snapshot bodies never appear here.

redaction_class.description: Declared redaction class reused from ADR-0007 / ADR-0010 redaction posture. Raw URLs, raw callback bodies, raw webhook bodies, raw delegated-token bodies, raw provider payloads, and raw imported-snapshot bodies never cross this boundary regardless of class.

trust_posture.description: ADR-0001 workspace-trust state the sync-health view was authored under.

non_negative_integer.description: Non-negative integer count. Counts are always ≥ 0; a zero count is the typed signal that the row reports zero impacted objects of the named class.

provider_class.description: Frozen provider-class vocabulary reused from schemas/integration/browser_handoff_packet.schema.json. Every sync-health view names exactly one class so notifications, activity-center rows, exports, and audit events can quote the same class without a generic 'External' or 'Cloud' fallback.

object_class.description: Frozen provider-side object class vocabulary. Aligned with object_type_class in schemas/providers/provider_link_header.schema.json so a single sync-health view row reports counts for the same class the link header renders.

provider_instance_ref.description: Opaque ref to the provider-instance the view reports on; empty string when the view applies at provider-host granularity.

canonical_host.description: Canonical provider host. Required when current_mode_class is mirror_derived so the view names the canonical host alongside the mirror posture.

provider_identity.description: Provider-side identity the sync-health view is scoped to.

scope_ref.description: Opaque ref to the typed object scope this entry covers (provider_object_relation_record ref, import_session_record ref, or scope-resolution row ref).

object_scope_entry.description: One entry in the typed object scope the sync-health view reports on.

current_mode_class.description: Frozen sync-mode vocabulary. Surfaces render this verbatim; collapsing two modes into a single 'sync failed' label is forbidden. live = current and verified; delayed = held / deferred / backed off; partial = some objects materialised, some did not; replayed = replay rewrote state; denied = at least one verified delivery denied; offline = provider unreachable or account suspended; mirror_derived = state served through a self-hosted or organisation mirror.

failure_class.description: Frozen failure-class vocabulary. Required when current_mode_class is anything other than live; surfaces MUST quote this verbatim.

cursor_state_class.description: Frozen cursor-state vocabulary. Required on every sync-health view; a generic 'behind' or 'stuck' label is forbidden.

degraded_import_class.description: Frozen degraded-import vocabulary. Imported provider state MUST NOT masquerade as current canonical truth; every degraded import is rendered through one typed class.

escalation_surface_class.description: Frozen escalation-surface vocabulary. The sync-health view's escalation_path lists every member exactly once so the same typed mode and degraded-import language travels every consumer.

support_export_channel_class.description: Frozen export-channel class. Sync-health views ride support bundles, audit packets, migration notes, admin-assisted handoff packets, and object handoff packets without revealing raw credentials or tenant-private payloads.

has_prior_success.description: False is the explicit no_prior_success_event marker; the scope has never had a verified-and-applied sync. True requires event_ref and captured_at.

event_ref.description: Opaque ref to the provider_event_record whose verified-and-applied delivery was the most recent successful sync for the scope. Required when has_prior_success is true; empty string otherwise.

captured_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source for the last successful sync. Required when has_prior_success is true; empty string otherwise.

allOf.description: When has_prior_success is true, event_ref and captured_at MUST be non-empty.

last_success_event_block.description: Last verified-and-applied provider event for the scope.

operator_review_ref.description: Opaque ref to the operator review row when failure_class is unknown_failure_repair_required or replay_held.

last_failure_event_block.description: Last failed provider event for the scope. Required whenever current_mode_class is anything other than live.

cursor_ref.description: Opaque ref to the cursor / sequence state held by the broker for the scope (never a raw cursor token).

sequence_hint.description: Short structured hint such as the page number, monotonic sequence number, or freshness floor; never a raw provider cursor.

cursor_state_block.description: Typed cursor / reset state for the scope.

blast_radius_count_row.description: One row of the typed blast-radius counts. Every row reports counts for one provider object_class so operators can judge the blast radius of a provider issue without reading raw event logs.

degraded_import_entry.description: One typed degraded-import entry. Imported provider state MUST NOT masquerade as current canonical truth; every degraded import is rendered through one typed class with an explicit reviewable summary, an opaque scope ref, and a non-negative impacted count.

replayed_event_refs.description: Opaque refs to webhook_replay_record rows whose disposition rewrote, replayed, or rewound state for the scope.

delayed_event_refs.description: Opaque refs to provider_event_record rows whose envelope was held, deferred, or rate-limited.

denied_event_refs.description: Opaque refs to provider_event_record rows whose verification or policy denied the delivery.

duplicate_event_refs.description: Opaque refs to provider_event_record rows suppressed by the replay ledger as duplicates.

impacted_local_object_refs.description: Opaque refs to local provider-linked records (provider_object_relation_record, publish_later_queue_item_record, deferred_publish_queue_item_record, conflict-review records) the degraded sync window touched.

imported_overlay_refs.description: Opaque refs to import_session_record rows or imported-snapshot rows the view's degraded entries point at.

stale_header_refs.description: Opaque refs to provider_link_header rows whose local_or_provider_source is now mirror-derived, imported-snapshot-only, or whose freshness has degraded because of this sync window.

provider_linked_notification_refs.description: Opaque refs to the notifications, toasts, banners, or activity-center rows the view is the source of truth for.

correlation_refs_block.description: Replay-blast-radius correlation block. Every typed array is required (may be empty for live mode); a non-live view MUST populate at least one of replayed_event_refs, delayed_event_refs, denied_event_refs, or duplicate_event_refs.

label_ref.description: Opaque ref to the typed label row the surface renders. The label row MUST quote the typed current_mode_class and (when applicable) the typed degraded_import_class verbatim.

escalation_path_entry.description: One entry of the typed escalation path. Every sync-health view lists all five escalation_surface_class members exactly once so the same typed mode and degraded-import language travels every consumer.

redacts_raw_credentials.description: Schema-enforced const true. Sync-health views MUST NOT carry raw credentials onto any export channel.

redacts_tenant_private_payloads.description: Schema-enforced const true. Sync-health views MUST NOT carry raw tenant-private bodies onto any export channel; structured field summaries and opaque refs only.

origin_disclosure.description: Canonical Aureline origin the sync-health view was authored under.

policy_context.description: Policy context the sync-health view was authored under.

object_scope.description: Typed object scope the view reports on. An empty array is forbidden; the view names the (provider, object scope) pair.

last_failure_event.description: Required whenever current_mode_class is anything other than live; forbidden when current_mode_class is live.

blast_radius_counts.description: Typed blast-radius counts by object_class. At least one row is required even when current_mode_class is live; the explicit zero-count row names the object scope the live mode is reporting on.

degraded_import_entries.description: Typed degraded-import entries. MUST be empty when current_mode_class is live. partial mode requires at least one entry whose class is partial or backfilled. denied mode requires at least one entry whose class is host_mismatch or stale_permission. mirror_derived mode requires at least one entry whose class is mirror_derived.

escalation_path.description: Typed escalation path. Every sync-health view MUST list all five escalation_surface_class members exactly once so the same typed mode and degraded-import language travels every consumer.

support_export_channels.description: Typed export channels the sync-health view may ride. Every view MUST cite at least one export channel so support, audit, and migration consumers see the same record set.

forbids_generic_sync_failed_copy.description: Schema-enforced const true. Surfaces that read this record MAY NOT render a generic 'sync failed', 'last sync failed', 'out of date', or 'connection error' label whenever the record can name a narrower mode or degraded class. The contract pins the typed copy; this marker is the mechanical floor.

audit_event_refs.description: Opaque refs to the provider_handoff audit events emitted for this sync-health view (provider_event_accepted, provider_event_denied, provider_event_held, provider_event_replayed, import_session_started, import_session_completed, import_session_failed_partial, webhook_replay_applied_once, webhook_replay_deduped_noop, webhook_replay_held_pending_sequence).

allOf.description: live mode requires cursor_state_class cursor_current and an empty degraded_import_entries array.
partial mode requires at least one degraded_import_entry whose class is partial or backfilled.

denied mode whose failure_class is host_mismatch or tenant_switch requires at least one degraded_import_entry whose class is host_mismatch (previously-imported state may now be host-mismatched and MUST be labelled rather than rendered as live).
denied mode whose failure_class is stale_permission requires at least one degraded_import_entry whose class is stale_permission so the previously-imported state is not rendered as live.
mirror_derived mode requires at least one degraded_import_entry whose class is mirror_derived.
denied mode requires last_failure_event.failure_class in {signature_denied, policy_blocked, host_mismatch, tenant_switch, stale_permission}.
offline mode requires last_failure_event.failure_class in {network_unreachable, credential_expired_or_revoked, provider_unreachable}.
partial mode requires last_failure_event.failure_class in {partial_failure, missing_page_detected, rate_limited, stale_permission}.
Every sync-health view MUST list a toast_or_banner escalation entry.
Every sync-health view MUST list a detail_view escalation entry.
Every sync-health view MUST list a cli_inspection escalation entry.
Every sync-health view MUST list a support_export escalation entry.
Every sync-health view MUST list an audit_packet escalation entry.

provider_sync_health_view_record.description: Typed snapshot of one (connected provider, object scope) pair's current sync mode, last-success and last-failure provider-event references, cursor / reset state, blast-radius counts by object class, degraded-import entries, replay-blast-radius correlation refs, escalation path, and support-export channels. Aureline MAY NOT render a generic 'sync failed' label whenever the record can name a narrower mode or degraded class; surfaces MUST quote the typed current_mode_class, the typed failure_class (when present), and the typed degraded_import_class (when present) verbatim. The same record shape is reusable across code-host, issue / planning, CI / checks, docs / portal, artifact-registry, release-publisher, identity-provider, AI-provider, and managed-admin surfaces.
